title: Allow Button columns on Subitems
excerpt:
The ability to have one press buttons on subitems would be useful.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
The ability to have one press buttons on subitems would be useful.

We have a scenario where subitems create scheduling items and they start with a certain number of hours. Setting the resources and timeline consumes hours. If those dont consume all the hours, having a button to trigger something (webhook, app, etc.) that takes the remaining hours and transfers to another subitem.

Buttons are the perfect way to do this, but for now i have to use a checkbox, and reset it to unchecked using integromat (along with do the actual actions needed).

Buttons would be most usable, i realized the issues is that you cant create automations on the subitem board directly. So if its a subitem just make it a dumb button and let us create the automations manually. Using a “column changed” trigger has the same effect as button pressed.

posts.author: higheredpmo (Sandy Bury)
posts.cookedHtml: We would like the button column to be available in subitems. We have a use case that was stopped short because of this limitation.

posts.author: MortenN (Morten Nielsen)
posts.cookedHtml: For a bunch of one-click actions, to trigger automations or in the context of integrations to software such as Make, the button would be super useful on subitems. Completely agree that this would be a nice addition to have

posts.author: rosmith2896 (Rochelle)
posts.cookedHtml: Would also love this feature. Not sure why it’s not there.

posts.author: ktraina (KT)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any plan to make this option available? We could really use this for our use case as we manage at the subitem status and this is a great way to create a simple automation of notifying people on that item when you are done. Please, please!!

posts.author: MeetBettyMKD (Marcus)
posts.cookedHtml: Also requesting this feature, would remove A LOT of manual workarounds for us. Remember: vote for this feature at the top of the page if you would also find this useful.

posts.author: vmccoy (V)
posts.cookedHtml: Highly needed item, I use subitems to list specific activities that need to be completed as a part of our overall project (item level) and often need to add roles/faculty to those activities. I end up having to make lots of columns to list all the faculty connected to an activity (subitem). If I had a button, I could use this to create items on our tracking faculty board based on just 2 columns data and have it link to that activity (subitem) under this board. Cleaning up the data. Please add this!
